plot: 
```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lattice)
library(RColorBrewer)

raw_df <- data.frame(
  motif = c("motif9", "motif5", "motif10", "motif11",
            "motif9", "motif5", "motif10", "motif11"),
  
  group = c("PB-seq", "PB-seq", "PB-seq", "PB-seq",
            "Random", "Random", "Random", "Random"),
  
  motif_positive = c(7799, 4361, 2316, 8913,
                     738, 968, 1522, 1554),
  
  total_regions = c(102830, 102830, 102830, 102830,
                    85474, 85474, 85474, 85474),
  
  mast_cutoff = c("0.00005",
                  "0.0001",
                  "0.000005",
                  "0.0001",
                  "0.00005",
                  "0.0001",
                  "0.000005",
                  "0.0001")
)

raw_df <- raw_df %>%
  mutate(
    motif_negative = total_regions - motif_positive,
    positive_fraction = motif_positive / total_regions,
    negative_fraction = motif_negative / total_regions
  )
  
# raw_df
    motif  group motif_positive total_regions mast_cutoff motif_negative
1  motif9 PB-seq           7799        102830     0.00005          95031
2  motif5 PB-seq           4361        102830      0.0001          98469
3 motif10 PB-seq           2316        102830    0.000005         100514
4 motif11 PB-seq           8913        102830      0.0001          93917
5  motif9 Random            738         85474     0.00005          84736
6  motif5 Random            968         85474      0.0001          84506
7 motif10 Random           1522         85474    0.000005          83952
8 motif11 Random           1554         85474      0.0001          83920
  positive_fraction negative_fraction
1       0.075843625         0.9241564
2       0.042409803         0.9575902
3       0.022522610         0.9774774
4       0.086677040         0.9133230
5       0.008634205         0.9913658
6       0.011325081         0.9886749
7       0.017806584         0.9821934
8       0.018180967         0.9818190
```


```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
motifs <- data.frame(
  motif = c("motif9", "motif5", "motif10", "motif11"),
  pb_positive = c(7799, 4361, 2316, 8913),
  pb_total = c(102830, 102830, 102830, 102830),
  random_positive = c(738, 968, 1522, 1554),
  random_total = c(85474, 85474, 85474, 85474)
)

motifs$p_value <- NA
motifs$odds_ratio <- NA
motifs$pb_fraction <- motifs$pb_positive / motifs$pb_total
motifs$random_fraction <- motifs$random_positive / motifs$random_total
motifs$fold_enrichment <- motifs$pb_fraction / motifs$random_fraction

for (i in 1:nrow(motifs)) {
  
  test_mat <- matrix(
    c(
      motifs$pb_positive[i],
      motifs$pb_total[i] - motifs$pb_positive[i],
      motifs$random_positive[i],
      motifs$random_total[i] - motifs$random_positive[i]
    ),
    nrow = 2,
    byrow = TRUE
  )
  
  res <- fisher.test(test_mat, alternative = "greater")
  
  motifs$p_value[i] <- res$p.value
  motifs$odds_ratio[i] <- res$estimate
}

motifs$FDR <- p.adjust(motifs$p_value, method = "BH")

motifs
```

 motif pb_positive pb_total random_positive random_total     p_value
1  motif9        7799   102830             738        85474 0.00000e+00
2  motif5        4361   102830             968        85474 0.00000e+00
3 motif10        2316   102830            1522        85474 2.47294e-13
4 motif11        8913   102830            1554        85474 0.00000e+00
  odds_ratio pb_fraction random_fraction fold_enrichment         FDR
1   9.422448  0.07584363     0.008634205        8.784089 0.00000e+00
2   3.866145  0.04240980     0.011325081        3.744768 0.00000e+00
3   1.270947  0.02252261     0.017806584        1.264847 2.47294e-13
4   5.124773  0.08667704     0.018180967        4.767460 0.00000e+00


```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
plot_df <- raw_df %>%
  select(motif, group, motif_positive, motif_negative, total_regions, mast_cutoff) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(motif_positive, motif_negative),
    names_to = "motif_status",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    fraction = count / total_regions,
    motif_status = recode(
      motif_status,
      motif_positive = "With motif",
      motif_negative = "Without motif"
    ),
    motif_status = factor(motif_status, levels = c("With motif", "Without motif")),
    group = factor(group, levels = c("PB-seq", "Random")),
    motif = factor(motif, levels = c("motif5", "motif9", "motif10", "motif11"))
  )
plot_df



```


```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
pdf('barplot_mast_fraction_PBpeak_vsRandom.pdf',width=16,height=6)
print(
barchart(
  fraction ~ group | motif,
  groups = motif_status,
  data = plot_df,
  stack = TRUE,
  layout = c(4, 1), 
  auto.key = list(
      space = "right",
      lines = FALSE,
      points = FALSE,
      rectangles = TRUE,
      cex = 1,
      reverse.rows = TRUE
    ),
  ylab = "Fraction of regions",
  xlab = "",
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  scales = list(
    x = list(rot = 45),
    y = list(at = seq(0, 1, 0.2))
  ),
  par.settings = list(
    superpose.polygon = list(
      col = c("black", "white"),
      border = "gray40"
    )
  ),
  panel = function(x, y, ...) {
    panel.barchart(x, y, ...)
  }
))
dev.off()
```



<!--
## #################

## GLAM2-all-round1-100bp-top5000
working directory: /home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/Exhaustive_denovomotif_analysis_260410/new_Exaust/GLAM2/glam2_all_PB_100bp_top5000 \

```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load bedtools
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
dir=/home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/GATA_Deseq2/peakcall0.05/

# extend the peak region to 161bp window centered on the summit
slopBed -b 50 -i ${dir}GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity.bed -g $sizes  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2n > GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.bed #102830

# convert to FASTA
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.bed -fo GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.fasta #205660
```


```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_top5000.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_top5000.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_top5000.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load meme/5.5.9

sort -nrk4,4 GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.bed | head -n 5000 > GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow_top5000.bed -fo GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow_top5000.fasta


# all PB-seq peaks with 101 window
glam2 -n 1000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_all_101win_glam2_top5000_output -2 n GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow_top5000.fasta
```

Conduct a TOMTOM to compare the GLAM2-round1 top motif with the 12 ChIP-seq motif: \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: "chip_12_motifs.meme"

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_all_101win_glam2_top5000_output/glam2.meme
mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif1_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round1 -eps -thresh 0.05 Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif1_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

head -n 3 tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round1/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	Overlap	Query_consensus	Target_consensuOrientation
#1	AGATDDDNAGATAAD	1	2.08745e-06	2.50494e-05	5.00988e-05	14	TTATCTATCTATCTAT	TTTATCTTTTCATCT	-
#1	21-AGATAAGG	2	0.0002405	0.002886	0.002886	6	TTATCTATCTATCTAT	CCTTATCT	-


```

Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif AGATDDDNAGATAAD as the closest match to the top GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round1/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)


# Best TOMTOM Target_ID: AGATDDDNAGATAAD
#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif3_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt #21361

Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed #21358

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round1.bed #208

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round1.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round1.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round1.fasta

#>chr1:13993520-13993535|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:13993520-13993535(+)
#AGATTGAATGGTAGT
#>chr1:27833840-27833855|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:27833840-27833855(+)
#AGATAACCAGGTATT
#>chr1:49964531-49964546|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:49964531-49964546(+)
#TGATGTCAGGATTGT
#>chr1:53431945-53431960|p=9.33e-05|strand=+::chr1:53431945-53431960(+)
#AGATCAATGGATGAG
#>chr1:58458949-58458964|p=9.33e-05|strand=+::chr1:58458949-58458964(+)
#tgatattaaggtaga

# Not all sequences near the cutoff perfectly match the motif core 3mer GAT

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt

Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed #19947

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005_round1.bed #385

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005_round1.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005_round1.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005_round1.fasta

#>chr1:5603392-5603407|p=4.55e-05|strand=-::chr1:5603392-5603407(-)
#agatatagagatgga
#>chr1:10778230-10778245|p=4.27e-05|strand=-::chr1:10778230-10778245(-)
#agattgtgtgattgt
#>chr1:20450313-20450328|p=4.27e-05|strand=+::chr1:20450313-20450328(+)
#tGATtgcaagattgt
#>chr1:21425737-21425752|p=4.84e-05|strand=-::chr1:21425737-21425752(-)
#TGATATTGGGATAAT
#>chr1:21482250-21482265|p=4.84e-05|strand=+::chr1:21482250-21482265(+)
#TGATATTGGGATAAT

# now looks much much better
```



```{r engine='R', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_101bpwindow.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed #82881
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta

```

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00001_round2.bed #107

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00001_round2.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00001_round2.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00001_round2.fasta
#>chr1:46588004-46588017|p=9.43e-06|strand=-::chr1:46588004-46588017(-)
#gaattagagataa
#>chr1:47003813-47003826|p=9.43e-06|strand=+::chr1:47003813-47003826(+)
#tgagcagagataa
#>chr1:69627856-69627869|p=9.43e-06|strand=+::chr1:69627856-69627869(+)
#taatgtaagataa
#>chr1:71766251-71766264|p=9.43e-06|strand=+::chr1:71766251-71766264(+)
#agatgtaagaaaa
#>chr1:81097976-81097989|p=9.43e-06|strand=+::chr1:81097976-81097989(+)
#aaatcaatgataa


# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.000005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt

Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed #1933

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.000004 && p <= 0.000005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > mast_hits_near_0.000005_round2.bed #

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.000005_round2.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.000005_round2.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.000005_round2.fasta

#>chr1:68832621-68832634|p=4.18e-06|strand=+::chr1:68832621-68832634(+)
#agagcaaagataa
#>chr1:72804036-72804049|p=4.18e-06|strand=-::chr1:72804036-72804049(-)
#agagcaaagataa
#>chr1:86484840-86484853|p=4.18e-06|strand=+::chr1:86484840-86484853(+)
#AGATGGGAGAAAA
#>chr1:88013128-88013141|p=4.8e-06|strand=-::chr1:88013128-88013141(-)
#agatgctagataa
#>chr1:93117534-93117547|p=4.43e-06|strand=+::chr1:93117534-93117547(+)
#AGATTTATGATAA
```



```{r engine='R', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed #80948
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.fasta

```

## GLAM2-all-round3-100bp-top5000

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_top5000_round3.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_top5000_round3.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_top5000_round3.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load meme/5.5.9

sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.fasta


# all PB-seq peaks with 101 window
glam2 -n 1000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_all_without_mot12_101win_glam2_top5000_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.fasta
```

the top motif is not a spaced gata3-like motif. \

re-run GLAM2 with double the "maximum number of iterations without improvement" \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_top5000_round3_1.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_top5000_round3_1.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_top5000_round3_1.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load meme/5.5.9

sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.fasta


# all PB-seq peaks (without motif1 and 2) with 101 window
glam2 -n 2000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_all_without_mot12_101win_glam2_top5000_output1 -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_top5000.fasta
```

increasing the "maximum number of iterations without improvement" seems improved the motif detection. Although the first motif still not a good GATA3 spaced motif (it is a ATC-ATG form), the second motif did show a GATA3 tandem-10 motif. I will use this tandem-10 motif as the round3 identified motif. \

Conduct a TOMTOM to compare the GLAM2-round3 motif with the 12 ChIP-seq motif: \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: "chip_12_motifs.meme"

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_all_without_mot12_101win_glam2_top5000_output1/glam2.meme
#mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 2_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif3_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round3 -eps -thresh 0.05 Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif3_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

head -n 3 tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round3/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	Overlap	Query_consensus	Target_consensuOrientation
#2	YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT	1	1.18191e-08	1.41829e-07	2.83658e-07	16	TTATCTTTTATAATCT	CTTATCTTTTCCAATCT	+
#2	21-AGATAAGG	2	0.000128143	0.00153772	0.00153772	TTATCTTTTATAATCT	CCTTATCT

```

Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT as the closest match to the top GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round3/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)


# Best TOMTOM Target_ID: YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT
# echo $matched_file
# /home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif8_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.txt #7317

Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.bed #7314

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round3.bed #273

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round3.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round3.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round3.fasta

#>chr1:11140819-11140836|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr1:11140819-11140836(-)
#TTTATCAGTGATAGTCA
#>chr1:42821386-42821403|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr1:42821386-42821403(-)
#GATATCACTCATAATCG
#>chr1:49044654-49044671|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr1:49044654-49044671(-)
#ataatccaggttaatct
#>chr1:50574194-50574211|p=9.75e-05|strand=+::chr1:50574194-50574211(+)
#TTTATCAGTTTTCATTG
#>chr1:65071129-65071146|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr1:65071129-65071146(-)
#GTAATCAATAAACATTT

# Not all sequences near the cutoff perfectly match the motif core 3mer GAT

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.txt #

Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.bed #5438

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005_round3.bed #

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005_round3.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005_round3.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005_round3.fasta

#>chr1:28449421-28449438|p=4.78e-05|strand=+::chr1:28449421-28449438(+)
#TTAATCCTGTTTTATCT
#>chr1:31912257-31912274|p=4.52e-05|strand=-::chr1:31912257-31912274(-)
#ATAATCTTCAATTATCT
#>chr1:33294209-33294226|p=4.02e-05|strand=+::chr1:33294209-33294226(+)
#attatctgatgttatct
#>chr1:34222374-34222391|p=4.78e-05|strand=-::chr1:34222374-34222391(-)
#tcaatccccctaaatct
#>chr1:43911604-43911621|p=4.26e-05|strand=-::chr1:43911604-43911621(-)
#TATATCTGTAGATATCT


```



```{r engine='R', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 12. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round3.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.bed #75510
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta
```

## GLAM2-all-round1
working directory: /home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/Exhaustive_denovomotif_analysis_260410/new_Exaust/GLAM2/glam2_all_PB

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks with 161 window
glam2 -O GATA_PB_all_161win_glam2_output -2 n GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta

#glam2 -Q -n 1000000 -z $minsites -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O $meme_out -2 n $.fasta
```

Conduct a TOMTOM to compare the GLAM2-round1 top motif with the 12 ChIP-seq motif: \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file
out="chip_12_motifs.meme"
meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
first_file=$(ls "$meme_dir"*meme.txt | sort | head -n 1)
awk '/^MOTIF/ {exit} {print}' "$first_file" > "$out"

for f in $(ls "$meme_dir"*meme.txt | sort)
do
    awk '
    BEGIN {keep=0}
    /^MOTIF/ {keep=1}
    keep == 1 {print}
    ' "$f" >> "$out"

    echo "" >> "$out"
done

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_all_161win_glam2_output/glam2.meme
mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif1_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif1_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	      Optimal_offset	p-value	    E-value	      q-value	    Overlap   Query_consensus	      Target_consensus	  Orientation
#1	AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	  -1	            0.000560288	0.00672346	  0.0134469	  19	      TAGATAAAAAAAAAAGATAA	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	+
#1	TTATCTBYNNVATCT	      -5	            0.00260378	0.0312454	    0.0312454	  15	      TAGATAAAAAAAAAAGATAA	AGATGAAAGAGATAA	    -
#1	21-AGATAAGG	          -14	            0.00461196	0.0553435	    0.0368957	            TAGATAAAAAAAAAAGATAA	AGATAAGG	          +

```

**A coherence check for using TOMTOM:** \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# negative control: TOMTOM the neg top glam2 motif against the 12 chip motif
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i ../GATA_PB_negcontrol_ran5000_161win_glam2_output/glam2.meme
mv 1_meme.txt neg_glam2.meme
rm *meme.txt

tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_neg -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson neg_glam2.meme chip_12_motifs.meme
cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_neg/tomtom.tsv
# no matching output


# positive control: chip motif with itself
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_pos -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson ${meme_dir}GATAmotif6_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme
cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_pos/tomtom.tsv

#Query_ID	            Target_ID	          Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	0	              1.34099e-28	1.60918e-27	3.21837e-27	19	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	+
#AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	RATCWWGATAA	        -8	            0.00374824	0.0449789	0.0333753	11	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	GATCTAGATAA	+
#AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	TTATCTBYNNVATCT	    0	              0.00417191	0.0500629	0.0333753	15	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	AGATGAAAGAGATAA	-
#AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA	AGATGRVAGATAA	      -6	            0.00566843	0.0680212	0.0340106	13	AGATGTCAAATACAGATAA	AGATGAGAGAT
```


Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif AGATBNNNNNNVNWGATAA as the closest match to the top GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load deeptools/3.5.0
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)



mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt #29658

# coherence check
# mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta | wc -l  # 29658 - 3 of 102830
# mast -mt 0.0005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta | wc -l  # 50285 - 3 of 102830

# random peak regions: /home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/260407_NB551647_0123_AH57FJBGYX/Data/Intensities/BaseCalls/GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final.bed #85474
# slopBed -b 80 -i /home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/260407_NB551647_0123_AH57FJBGYX/Data/Intensities/BaseCalls/GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final.bed -g $sizes  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2n > GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final_161window.bed #85474
# fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final_161window.bed -fo GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final_161window.fasta
# mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final_161window.fasta | wc -l # 1705
# mast -mt 0.0005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file GATA_PB_neg3_summits_final_161window.fasta | wc -l # 11858


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed #29655

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001.bed #269

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001.fasta

#>chr1:21062541-21062560|p=9.3e-05|strand=+::chr1:21062541-21062560(+)
#AGACGTAAGCAGTAGATAT
#>chr1:43412813-43412832|p=9.89e-05|strand=+::chr1:43412813-43412832(+)
#tgattttaaaatgggattt
#>chr1:45972790-45972809|p=9.01e-05|strand=-::chr1:45972790-45972809(-)
#agacagatcttgctgataa
#>chr1:60583313-60583332|p=9.6e-05|strand=-::chr1:60583313-60583332(-)
#tgacaataataattgataa
#>chr1:74549574-74549593|p=9.3e-05|strand=-::chr1:74549574-74549593(-)
#AGACAGATACTTGAGATAA

# Not all sequences near the cutoff perfectly match the motif

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt #28371

# coherence check
# mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.fasta | wc -l  # 28371 - 3 of 102830


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed #28368

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005.bed #455

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005.fasta

#>chr1:5071571-5071590|p=4.4e-05|strand=-::chr1:5071571-5071590(-)
#tgataaagaaatttgatta
#>chr1:31807489-31807508|p=4.6e-05|strand=+::chr1:31807489-31807508(+)
#tgataaaatattgagattg
#>chr1:35310402-35310421|p=4e-05|strand=+::chr1:35310402-35310421(+)
#TGATAGTAATTTTTGATAC
#>chr1:40140441-40140460|p=4.2e-05|strand=+::chr1:40140441-40140460(+)
#tgataatatttacagattc
#>chr1:42226835-42226854|p=4.4e-05|strand=-::chr1:42226835-42226854(-)
#AGATCTCAAACTGGGATTA

# now looks much much better
```



```{r engine='R', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a ../GATA_PB_summits_final_DESeq2_ranked_intensity_161bpwindow.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round1.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed #74460
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta

```

## GLAM2-all-round2

```{r engine='R', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round2_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round2_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round2_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif1
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1_161win_glam2_round2_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta
```

Conduct TOMTOM \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1_161win_glam2_round2_output/glam2.meme
# mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif2_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round2 -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif2_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round2/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#1	AGATDDDNAGATAAD	-2	1.1925e-06	1.431e-05	2.862e-05	15	ATAGATTAAAAGATAAAGAT	AGATGAAAAGATAAA	+
#1	21-AGATAAGG	-10	0.000402817	0.00483381	0.00385503	ATAGATTAAAAGATAAAGAT	AGATAAGG	+
#1	AGATBYTTATC	-11	0.000481879	0.00578254	0.00385503	ATAGATTAAAAGATAAAGAT	GATAAAAATCT	-


```


Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif AGATDDDNAGATAAD as the closest match to the top round2_GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load deeptools/3.5.0
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round2/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)

#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif3_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt #18290


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed #18287

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round2.bed #182

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round2.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round2.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round2.fasta

# MOTIF	AGATDDDNAGATAAD
#>chr1:13993520-13993535|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:13993520-13993535(+)
#AGATTGAATGGTAGT
#>chr1:17004038-17004053|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:17004038-17004053(+)
#tgatagtctgattca
#>chr1:27833840-27833855|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:27833840-27833855(+)
#AGATAACCAGGTATT
#>chr1:49964531-49964546|p=9.77e-05|strand=+::chr1:49964531-49964546(+)
#TGATGTCAGGATTGT
#>chr1:58458949-58458964|p=9.33e-05|strand=+::chr1:58458949-58458964(+)
#tgatattaaggtaga

# Not all sequences near the cutoff perfectly match the motif

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt #17051


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed #17048 of 102830

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005_round2.bed #324

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005_round2.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005_round2.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005_round2.fasta
```

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}

intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_1.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round2.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed #57409

fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12.fasta
```

## GLAM2-all-round4

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round4_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round4_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round4_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_161win_glam2_round4_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta
```

This round of GLAM2 generated a top GATA3-like motif, tandem-12, but the motif structure does not look very strong. One of the half-sites appears less promising.\





## GLAM2-all-round4-1 (time-consuming parameters added)

I decided to modify the glam2 command by modifying on some parameters: \
`-n` Specify how many iterations should pass since the highest-scoring alignment seen so far before ending each alignment run. \
`-z` Specify the minimum number sequences that must participate in the alignment. If greater than the number of input sequences, then all the sequences must participate.	\
`-a`	minimum aligned;	Specify the minimum number of key positions (aligned columns) in the alignment.	\
`-b`	maximum aligned;	Specify the maximum number of key positions (aligned columns) in the alignment.\
`-w`  aligned columns; 	Specify the initial number of key positions (aligned columns) in the alignment. If less than the minimum (-a) or greater than the maximum (-b), it is increased to the minimum or reduced to the maximum. \


```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round4_all_1.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round4_all_1.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round4_all_1.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
glam2 -n 1000000 -z 1000000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_161win_glam2_round4_output1 -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta

#glam2 -n 1000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_161win_glam2_round4_output2 -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta
```

This reports a top motif tandem-10. \


### alternatively try MEME

I also submitted a MEME job using the top 5,000 PB-seq peaks, after removing peaks containing the motifs identified in GLAM2 rounds 1, 2, and 3. I used this 5000-peak subset to reduce the MEME running time. \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round4_all_meme.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round4_all_meme.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round4_all_meme.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9


sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_top5000.fasta

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
meme -p 32 -oc GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_161win_meme_round4_output -nmotifs 10 -objfun classic -csites 20000 -searchsize 0 -minw 8 -maxw 30 -revcomp -dna -markov_order 2 -maxsize 100000000 PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_top5000.fasta
```

MEME on top5000 PB-seq peak (without motif1,2,3) identified a tandem-12 motif (looks better than GLAM2 original round4 top motif) as the top enriched motif (2978 sites of 5000); it also reports a convergent-8 as the 8th motif (321 sites of 5000). \

### Conduct TOMTOM

Because independent MEME and GLAM2 analyses both identified a tandem-12 GATA3 motif as the top enriched motif, I believe that the tandem-12 motif is enriched in the remaining PB-seq peaks after excluding peaks containing the round 1, 2, and 3 motifs. I am now conducting TOMTOM analysis to match this motif (glam2-round4 top) to the ChIP-seq motifs. \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
#module load deeptools/3.5.0
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif
#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12_161win_glam2_round4_output/glam2.meme
# mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round4 -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round4/tomtom.tsv
# Using a TOMTOM threshold of 0.05, we did not identify any significant matches between the GLAM2 round 4 top motif and the 12 ChIP-seq motifs. This may be because the GLAM2 round 4 top motif is relatively degenerate.


```

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# /home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/Exhaustive_denovomotif_analysis_260410/new_Exaust/GLAM2/glam2_all_PB/Glam2_all_individual_meme
module load meme/5.5.9
ceqlogo \
        -i1 Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_meme.txt \
        -o Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_rc.eps \
        -r
```

### Conduct TOMTOM-meme

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
#module load deeptools/3.5.0
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif
#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123_161win_meme_round4_output/meme.txt
# mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp MEME-1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_2_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round4_2 -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif4_2_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round4_2/tomtom.tsv
# Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#CTTATCTBWWWTHWRATCT	21-AGATAAGG	1	0.000156447	0.00187737	0.00375474	7	CTTATCTTTTTTAAAATCT	CCTTATCT	-
#CTTATCTBWWWTHWRATCT	NAGATBNNARNNVWGATA	-2	0.00119248	0.0143097	0.0143097	17	CTTATCTTTTTTAAAATCT	TATCTGTATTGCCATCTC	-
#CTTATCTBWWWTHWRATCT	NCTTATCWGAT	1	0.00448008	0.0537609	0.0358406	10	CTTATCTTTTTTAAAATCT	TCTTATCTGAT	+
#CTTATCTBWWWTHWRATCT	TTATCTBYNNVATCT	-1	0.00667381	0.0800858	0.0400429	15	CTTATCTTTTTTAAAATCT	TTATCTCTTTCATCT	+
#CTTATCTBWWWTHWRATCT	YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT	0	0.00838687	0.100640.040257	17	CTTATCTTTTTTAAAATCT	CTTATCTTTTCCAATCT	+
```



Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif NAGATBNNARNNVWGATA as the closest spaced match (ignoring the top being the single site motif) to the top round2_GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round3/tomtom.tsv"

best_target="NAGATBNNARNNVWGATA"

matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)

# Best TOMTOM Target_ID: NAGATBNNARNNVWGATA

#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif7_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.txt #19600


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.bed #19597

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round4.bed #194

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round4.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round4.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round4.fasta

#>chr1:10590504-10590522|p=9.21e-05|strand=+::chr1:10590504-10590522(+)
#CAGATAGAGAAACAGACA
#>chr1:74071625-74071643|p=9.21e-05|strand=+::chr1:74071625-74071643(+)
#gagataagatctttgatt
#>chr1:83173672-83173690|p=9.21e-05|strand=-::chr1:83173672-83173690(-)
#atgatacatgtgatgatt
#>chr1:90220361-90220379|p=9.8e-05|strand=+::chr1:90220361-90220379(+)
#ATGATAAGATCAGTGATT
#>chr1:106895043-106895061|p=9.21e-05|strand=-::chr1:106895043-106895061(-)
#aagataatttgtatgata

tail mast_hits_near_0.0001_round4.fasta
#>chrX:68601850-68601868|p=9.21e-05|strand=+::chrX:68601850-68601868(+)
#aagatgtaatttgtgaca
#>chrX:74449572-74449590|p=9.8e-05|strand=+::chrX:74449572-74449590(+)
#CAGATGCAATTGAAGACT
#>chrX:78862953-78862971|p=9.21e-05|strand=+::chrX:78862953-78862971(+)
#aagatctgcaagaagaca
#>chrX:90633655-90633673|p=9.21e-05|strand=-::chrX:90633655-90633673(-)
#aagatatatatatagatc
#>chrX:119388214-119388232|p=9.21e-05|strand=+::chrX:119388214-119388232(+)
#gagatctgtcccaagatc

# Not all sequences near the cutoff perfectly match the motif

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.txt #18500


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.bed #18497 of 102830

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005_round4.bed #540

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005_round4.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005_round4.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005_round4.fasta

#>chr1:25997130-25997148|p=4.28e-05|strand=+::chr1:25997130-25997148(+)
#AAGATACAGTCCAAGATA
#>chr1:26419200-26419218|p=4.28e-05|strand=+::chr1:26419200-26419218(+)
#CTGATTACCTATCTGATA
#>chr1:33294272-33294290|p=4.97e-05|strand=+::chr1:33294272-33294290(+)
#cagatgaggaaactgatg
#>chr1:38792393-38792411|p=4.28e-05|strand=+::chr1:38792393-38792411(+)
#ATgataagagcttagata
#>chr1:51056005-51056023|p=4.61e-05|strand=-::chr1:51056005-51056023(-)
#tagatcatatgttagatt

tail mast_hits_near_0.00005_round4.fasta
#>chrX:126961916-126961934|p=4.61e-05|strand=-::chrX:126961916-126961934(-)
#tggatcagaacaaagata
#>chrX:129582553-129582571|p=4.61e-05|strand=+::chrX:129582553-129582571(+)
#TAGATTCTTCATTTGATA
#>chrX:138979382-138979400|p=4.28e-05|strand=+::chrX:138979382-138979400(+)
#tagataaGATCAGAGATT
#>chrX:150621874-150621892|p=4.61e-05|strand=-::chrX:150621874-150621892(-)
#CTGATCCCTGCCTTGATA
#>chrX:152531332-152531350|p=4.61e-05|strand=-::chrX:152531332-152531350(-)
#ctgatcatgtggtagata

# now looks much much better, but tagataaGATCAGAGATT sequence could match to a tandem-5, tandem-7 and the tandem-12
```



```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1 and 2. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_123.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round4.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.bed #29173

fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.fasta

```


## GLAM2-all-round5
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round5_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round5_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round5_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234_161win_glam2_round5_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.fasta
```

the top motif looks like the convergent-9 motif. \

Conduct TOMTOM  \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234_161win_glam2_round5_output/glam2.meme
# mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp 1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif5_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round5 -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif5_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round5/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#1	AGATBYTTATC	-3	1.53377e-08	1.84052e-07	3.68104e-07	11	AAAGATAAAGATCTATAAAA	GATAAAAATCT	-


```


Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif AGATBYTTATC as the closest match to the top round2_GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round5/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)

# Best TOMTOM Target_ID: AGATBYTTATC

#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif2_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round5.txt #9105


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round5.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round5.bed #9102

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round5.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round5.bed #19

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round5.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round5.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round5.fasta

#>chr1:11493422-11493433|p=9.57e-05|strand=+::chr1:11493422-11493433(+)
#tgatgctgatc
#>chr10:5450647-5450658|p=9.57e-05|strand=-::chr10:5450647-5450658(-)
#TGATGAGAATC
#>chr10:55048691-55048702|p=9.3e-05|strand=-::chr10:55048691-55048702(-)
#tgataaatatC
#>chr12:82403228-82403239|p=9.3e-05|strand=-::chr12:82403228-82403239(-)
#TGATCCAAATC
#>chr12:115711246-115711257|p=9.57e-05|strand=-::chr12:115711246-115711257(-)
#TGATGAGAATC


# looks good
```



```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1 and 2. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round5.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.bed #20068

fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.fasta

```

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}

#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1

#SBATCH -p general

module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif12345
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345_161win_glam2_round6_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.fasta
```

This round generate a degenerate convergent-8 motif. \

Conduct TOMTOM  \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif

#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py

rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round6/tomtom.tsv
#Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#1	WGATAAVATCW	-9	7.83106e-06	9.39728e-05	0.000187946	11	AATAAATAAAGATAAGATCT	AGATAACATCT	+
#1	21-AGATAAGG	-9	0.000991448	0.0118974	0.0118974	AATAAATAAAGATAAGATCT	AGATAAGG	+


```


Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif WGATAAVATCW as the closest match to the top round2_GLAM2 motif. Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"
tomtom_tsv="tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round6/tomtom.tsv"

best_target=$(awk 'BEGIN{FS="\t"} 
    NR > 1 && $1 !~ /^#/ && NF >= 10 {
        print $2, $6
    }' "$tomtom_tsv" | sort -k2,2g | head -n 1 | awk '{print $1}')
echo "Best TOMTOM Target_ID: $best_target"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)

# Best TOMTOM Target_ID: WGATAAVATCW


#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif1_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.txt #8452


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.bed #8449

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round6.bed #16

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round6.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round6.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round6.fasta

#>chr10:17183156-17183167|p=9.8e-05|strand=+::chr10:17183156-17183167(+)
#AGATAACATCC
#>chr11:5691756-5691767|p=9.46e-05|strand=-::chr11:5691756-5691767(-)
#CGATAAGATCG
#>chr12:56579868-56579879|p=9.8e-05|strand=-::chr12:56579868-56579879(-)
#agataacatcc
#>chr12:73917128-73917139|p=9.14e-05|strand=+::chr12:73917128-73917139(+)
#GGATAAAATCG
#>chr16:59953889-59953900|p=9.46e-05|strand=+::chr16:59953889-59953900(+)
#GGATTTAATCA

cat mast_hits_near_0.0001_round6.fasta
# there are some GATT-ATC sequences

# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.txt #8389


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.txt
```

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1 and 2. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round6.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456.bed #12077
```

## GLAM2-all-round7

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round7_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round7_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round7_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123456
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_161win_glam2_round7_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456.fasta
```

degenerated Tandem-9, tandem-10, tandem-11

### alternatively try MEME

I also submitted a MEME job using the top 5,000 PB-seq peaks, after removing peaks containing the motifs identified in GLAM2 rounds 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. I used this 5000-peak subset to reduce the MEME running time. \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round7_all_meme.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round7_all_meme.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round7_all_meme.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9


sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_top5000.fasta

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
meme -p 32 -oc GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_161win_meme_round7_output -nmotifs 10 -objfun classic -csites 20000 -searchsize 0 -minw 8 -maxw 30 -revcomp -dna -markov_order 2 -maxsize 100000000 PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_top5000.fasta
```

Alternative MEME reports a Tandem-11 motif as the top one. \

I will prioritize this tandem-11 motif as the round7 motif. \

### Conduct TOMTOM-meme

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
module load meme/5.5.9
#module load deeptools/3.5.0
module load py-deeptools
module load bedtools
module load R/4.1.2
# concatenate 12 ChIP-seq motifs in one MEME file: chip_12_motifs.meme

# get the top Glam2 motif
#wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sysunn/siyu_daily_update/main/December_2023/MEME_individual_from_db_python3.py
python ../MEME_individual_from_db_python3_updated.py -i GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456_161win_meme_round7_output/meme.txt
# mkdir Glam2_all_individual_meme
cp MEME-1_meme.txt Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif7_2_meme.txt
rm *meme.txt


# TOMTOM
tomtom -oc tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round7_2 -thresh 0.05 -dist pearson Glam2_all_individual_meme/Glam2_PB_GATAmotif7_2_meme.txt chip_12_motifs.meme

cat tomtom_glam2_vs_chip12_qval_round7_2/tomtom.tsv
# Query_ID	Target_ID	Optimal_offset	p-value	E-value	q-value	OverlapQuery_consensus	Target_consensus	Orientation
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	21-AGATAAGG	0	8.00538e-05	0.000960646	0.00192129	8	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	CCTTATCT	-
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	TATCTBNNDDMVATCT	-3	0.000676992	0.0081239	0.0081239	16	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	TATCTTTAAAAAATCT	+
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT	-1	0.00405272	0.0486327	0.0316414	17	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	CTTATCTTTTCCAATCT	+
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	NCTTATCWGAT	0	0.00618671	0.0742405	0.0316414	11	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	TCTTATCTGAT	+
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	TTATCTBYNNVATCT	-4	0.00659196	0.0791035	0.0316414	15	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	TTATCTCTTTCATCT	+
#YCTTATCTHTHTWHRATCTWW	WGATAAVATCW	3	0.0110886	0.133063	0.0443544	8	TCTTATCTTTTTTAAATCTTT	AGATGTTATCT	-
```




Among the 12 ChIP-seq motif variants, TOMTOM ranked motif TATCTBNNDDMVATCT as the closest match to the top round2_GLAM2 motif (I ignored the top single-site motif here). Because the target database contained only 12 motifs, significance values were interpreted cautiously. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes

meme_dir="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/"

best_target="TATCTBNNDDMVATCT"


matched_file=$(awk -v target="$best_target" '
    /^MOTIF/ {
        if ($2 == target || $3 == target) {
            print FILENAME
            exit
        }
    }
' "$meme_dir"*meme.txt)




#echo $matched_file
#/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif9_meme.txt

mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_123456.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round7.txt #3140


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round7.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round7.bed #3137

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round7.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round7.bed #137

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round7.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round7.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round7.fasta
```

## GLAM2-all-round8

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round8_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round8_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round8_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif1234567
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_161win_glam2_round8_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.fasta
```

glam2 did not reports more useful GATA3 spaced motif. \

### GLAM2-all-round8-1

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round8_all_1.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round8_all_1.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round8_all_1.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif1234567
glam2 -n 1000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_161win_glam2_round8_output1 -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.fasta
```


### alternatively try MEME

I also submitted a MEME job using the top 5,000 PB-seq peaks, after removing peaks containing the motifs identified in GLAM2 rounds 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. I used this 5000-peak subset to reduce the MEME running time. \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round8_all_meme.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round8_all_meme.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round8_all_meme.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9


sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_top5000.fasta

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif123
meme -p 32 -oc GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_161win_meme_round8_output -nmotifs 10 -objfun classic -csites 20000 -searchsize 0 -minw 8 -maxw 30 -revcomp -dna -markov_order 2 -maxsize 100000000 PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567_top5000.fasta
```

MEME did not reports more useful GATA3 spaced motif. \

But I know that in Glam2 round4-1 (which used a slightly different glam2 command) and glam2-round7, there is a tandem-10 motif being found. So in round-8 I will exclude the **tandem-10** motif from remaining peaks. \

Mast using the closest matched ChIP-seq motif \
MAST - Given a motif position weight matrix, MAST can search sequences for the occurrence of that motif. \
Usage: http://meme-suite.org/tools/mast \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
sizes=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.chrom.sizes
matched_file="/home/FCAM/ssun/GATA3_ChIP_PRO_July2023/ChIP_final/re_run_exhaustive_denovomotif_260414/single_bash2/individual_meme/GATAmotif8_meme.txt"

# MOTIF	YTTATCTYYNNHVATCT
mast -mt 0.0001 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.txt #2198


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.bed #2195

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00009 && p <= 0.0001) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.bed > mast_hits_near_0.0001_round8.bed #36

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.0001_round8.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.0001_round8.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.0001_round8.fasta

#>chr10:53470675-53470692|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr10:53470675-53470692(-)
#aatatcagattagatct
#>chr10:90372111-90372128|p=9.31e-05|strand=-::chr10:90372111-90372128(-)
#tctatcaacatttatca
#>chr12:9859943-9859960|p=9.75e-05|strand=+::chr12:9859943-9859960(+)
#agtatctcaatagatca
#>chr12:129562892-129562909|p=9.31e-05|strand=-::chr12:129562892-129562909(-)
#cttatctgatttggtca
#>chr12:130012914-130012931|p=9.75e-05|strand=-::chr12:130012914-130012931(-)
#tatatctctatccgtct


# change to a more stringent mast_p cut off
mast -mt 0.00005 -hit_list -best  $matched_file PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.fasta > mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.txt #1977


Rscript /home/FCAM/ssun/scripts/updated_parse_mast_to_coordinates.R mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.txt 
wc -l mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.bed #1974

# coherence check: check the near mast_cutoff sequences -- evaluate if they are acceptable / match to the spaced motif
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
awk 'BEGIN{OFS="\t"}
{
    p = $5
    if (p >= 0.00004 && p <= 0.00005) {
        name = $1 ":" $2 "-" $3 "|p=" $5 "|strand=" $6
        print $1, $2, $3, name, $5, $6
    }
}' mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.bed > mast_hits_near_0.00005_round8.bed #60

module load bedtools
bedtools getfasta -fi $genome -bed mast_hits_near_0.00005_round8.bed -fo mast_hits_near_0.00005_round8.fasta -name -s
head mast_hits_near_0.00005_round8.fasta

#>chr1:72229381-72229398|p=4.78e-05|strand=-::chr1:72229381-72229398(-)
#AATATCATTTAAAATCA
#>chr1:104808761-104808778|p=4.26e-05|strand=-::chr1:104808761-104808778(-)
#tatatctatatatatct
#>chr1:112104353-112104370|p=4.02e-05|strand=-::chr1:112104353-112104370(-)
#ataatctaatgtaatct
#>chr1:162728029-162728046|p=4.26e-05|strand=+::chr1:162728029-162728046(+)
#tatatctatatatatct
#>chr10:55234879-55234896|p=4.26e-05|strand=+::chr10:55234879-55234896(+)
#ATTATCACAGCTGATCA


# looks much better
```



```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
# Use `intersectBed` to find GATA3 peaks without motif 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. And convert to FASTA. 
intersectBed -v -a PB_without_glam2_motifs_1234567.bed -b mast_GATA3_PSWM_in_all_PB_peaks_glam2_round8.bed > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.bed #8403

fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.fasta

```


## GLAM2-all-round9

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round9_all.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 1
#SBATCH -c 32
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round9_all.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round9_all.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif1234567
glam2 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_161win_glam2_round9_output -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.fasta

glam2  -n 1000000 -z 1000 -a 8 -b 30 -w 16 -O GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_161win_glam2_round9_output1 -2 n PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.fasta
```

no more GATA3-like spaced motif found. \

### alternatively try MEME

I also submitted a MEME job using the top 5,000 PB-seq peaks, after removing peaks containing the motifs identified in GLAM2 rounds 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8. I used this 5000-peak subset to reduce the MEME running time. \

```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Glam2_round9_all_meme.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o Glam2_round9_all_meme.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e Glam2_round9_all_meme.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9


sort -nrk4,4 PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678.bed | head -n 5000 > PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.bed #5000

#Convert this top 5000 peak BED file to a FASTA file with `fastaFromBed`. 
module load bedtools
genome=/home/FCAM/ssun/Genome/hg38.fa
fastaFromBed -fi $genome -bed PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.bed -fo PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.fasta

# all PB-seq peaks without glam2_found motif12345678
meme -p 32 -oc GATA_PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_161win_meme_round9_output -nmotifs 10 -objfun classic -csites 20000 -searchsize 0 -minw 8 -maxw 30 -revcomp -dna -markov_order 2 -maxsize 100000000 PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.fasta
```

Except for the single GATAA site being identified as the 9th of 10 motifs, we did not see more spaced GATA3 motifs using the above command. \

I decided to try additional MEME with different parameter settings. \

working directory: dir=/home/FCAM/ssun/PBseq/Exhaustive_denovomotif_analysis_260410/new_Exaust/MEME

Glam2_round9_all_MEME_exaust.sh \
```{r, engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=top5000_win161_meme_ws_XXXXXX_wg_YYYYYY_ZZZZZZ_r9.sh
#SBATCH -N 1
#SBATCH -n 32
#SBATCH -c 1
#SBATCH -p general
#SBATCH --qos=general
#SBATCH --mem=128G
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=ssun@uchc.edu
#SBATCH -o top5000_win161_meme_ws_XXXXXX_wg_YYYYYY_ZZZZZZ_r9.sh_%j.out
#SBATCH -e top5000_win161_meme_ws_XXXXXX_wg_YYYYYY_ZZZZZZ_r9.sh_%j.err

hostname
export TMPDIR="${HOME}/temp"
module load py-deeptools
module load meme/5.5.9

ncore=32
e_thresh=.05
objfun=classic
minsites=$(awk '/^>/{count++} END{print int(count*0.2)}' PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.fasta) #1000, 20% of my input sequences

test_ws=XXXXXX
test_wg=YYYYYY
test_order=ZZZZZZ

meme -p $ncore -oc GATA3_without_mot_12345678_161bp_top5000_ws_XXXXXX_wg_YYYYYY_order_ZZZZZZ_meme_output -evt $e_thresh -csites 20000 -objfun $objfun -minw 8 -maxw 12 -wg $test_wg -ws $test_ws -searchsize 0 -revcomp -dna -markov_order $test_order -minsites $minsites PB_without_glam2_motifs_12345678_top5000.fasta
          
```

parallel running: \
```{r engine='bash', eval=F, echo=TRUE}
sh_file=Glam2_round9_all_MEME_exaust.sh

wg_value=("0" "1" "11")
ws_value=("0" "0.5" "1")
order=("0" "1" "2" "3")


for test_ws in "${ws_value[@]}"
do
    echo $test_ws
    for test_wg in "${wg_value[@]}"
    do
      echo $test_wg
      for test_order in "${order[@]}"
      do
       echo $test_order
       sed -e "s/XXXXXX/${test_ws}/g" -e "s/YYYYYY/${test_wg}/g" -e "s/ZZZZZZ/${test_order}/g" "$sh_file" > top5000_win161_meme_ws_${test_ws}_wg_${test_wg}_${test_order}_r9.sh  
      sbatch top5000_win161_meme_ws_${test_ws}_wg_${test_wg}_${test_order}_r9.sh  
      sleep 1
      done
    done
done
```

In the above parellel running slurm jobs, I tested different combinations between wg/ws values (gap penalty values) and the markov_order up to 3 (because the core elements for GATA3 motifs are the GAT which is a 3mer or GATA which is a 4mer). \

at a time I can run 10 slurm jobs with the request of 32 cpus per job. I will check the outputs once they are completed. \

Checked all MEME results, and only GATAA single GATA3 motif is found. \

0_11_3 GATAA single site \

0.5_11_3 GATAA single site \

1_11_3 GATAA single site \
